steps.title: Identify the time marker
steps.content: The phrase "wouldn't come until decades later" signals a jump forward in time from 1929 to a much later discovery. This time reference is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is sequence in time. Sentence 1 establishes that scientists in 1929 assumed progesterone existed only in animals; Sentence 2 reveals a future discovery disproving that assumption. The blank needs a transition signaling forward movement in time to a later development.
  • Choice A — "In other words,": signals that Sentence 2 restates Sentence 1 in different terms — but Sentence 2 introduces a new discovery, not a paraphrase. ✗
  • Choice B — "For example,": signals that Sentence 2 is a specific instance of the general claim in Sentence 1 — but finding progesterone in corn contradicts the assumption, not illustrates it. ✗
  • Choice C — "Eventually,": signals forward movement in time to a later point — matching the phrase "wouldn't come until decades later." ✓
  • Choice D — "Earlier,": signals a time before Sentence 1 — but the corn plant discovery happened after 1929, not before. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
